comments.post_body:
Encerro um ciclo de 13 anos na Microsoft, uma jornada que me trouxe aprendizados imensos, grandes realizações e a oportunidade de viver o sonho de trabalhar em uma das maiores empresas de tecnologia do mundo. Tive experiências que ultrapassaram fronteiras, colaborando com clientes e colegas em projetos no Brasil e no exterior. Foi um privilégio participar na linha de frente de momentos marcantes e escrever uma parte da história da Microsoft, como a liderança em Inteligência Artificial, o fortalecimento e expansão do Azure e a evolução da engenharia de software através do Visual Studio, Azure DevOps e posteriormente com GitHub, impactando centenas de milhares de desenvolvedores ao longo desses anos nos quais tive oportunidade de visitar, conhecer e conversar.

Nada disso teria sido possível sem as pessoas incríveis que estiveram ao meu lado nessa trajetória. Sou profundamente grato a todos que me apoiaram, inspiraram e me ensinaram em cada projeto, conversa e desafio. Além de colegas, fiz amigos para a vida toda e sou grato por cada história vivida juntos.

Chegou o momento de iniciar uma nova jornada, ela será interessante e diferente do que já trabalhei, uma boa mudança que trará experiência complementar de tudo que vivi nesses 13 anos de Microsoft, mas essas novidades deixarei para uma próxima história (post)!

Christiano Faig Alessandro Loesch Jannuzzi Stéfany Mazon Carlos Abramo Paula Silva Gustavo Zimmermann Lalo (Eduardo) Steinmann Carlos Zimmermann Fernando Filiputti Iury Oliveira Livia Regina Bijelli Ferreira João Paulo Fernandes Vinícius Souza Marcel Aldecoa Allan Targino Fernando de Oliveira Fabrício Catae Luiz Ávila Junior Daniel Consolim Oliveira João Carlos Bolonha Flavio Franco Madson Menezes Marden Menezes Richard Dalceno Chaves Fabio Hara Leandro Silveira Prado Wanderson Lima Rodrigo Benin Ribeiro Jeremiah Talkar David Makogon Lucas Camargo Reis Andresa Silveira Nataly Almeida Thiago Ferreira Kleumer Siqueira Kadu Carlos Eduardo Soares Renato Manzan Gianlucca Bigarelli Ednei Monteiro Hao Luo

I’ll keep it short — like many others, I was impacted by the layoffs yesterday, and my role has been eliminated. I'm still processing everything, but I’m staying optimistic and looking forward to the new opportunities ahead.

I want to sincerely thank all my colleagues at Microsoft for an incredible experience — for your support, mentorship, and collaboration. Who knows, maybe our paths will cross again in the future! 😊 

If you have a moment and would like to leave feedback about me or my work, please don’t hesitate. I’m not sure how often those are reviewed these days, but it certainly can’t hurt.

Wishing you all the best!

comments.comment_body: Thank you, Matheus Tait. Great day... even better after this tremendous allyship demonstration.
comments.post_body:
𝐀𝐥𝐥𝐲𝐬𝐡𝐢𝐩 𝐢𝐧 𝐋𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐡𝐢𝐩

Allyship starts with understanding that biases exist. Both personal and systemic. And then taking action. 

As leaders, we must dismantle inequality and foster inclusion. 

𝐈𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐥 𝐖𝐨𝐦𝐞𝐧'𝐬 𝐃𝐚𝐲 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐃𝐄𝐈:

Yet, on this International Women's Day 2025, I'm disappointed by the backlash against DEI initiatives. 

I get it: traditional approaches haven't always worked, and businesses  now need to focus on financial resilience.  That's all cool.

But...

While some specific DEI initiatives may be under scrutiny, the core mission remains even more critical: 𝘤𝘳𝘦𝘢𝘵𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘸𝘰𝘳𝘬𝘱𝘭𝘢𝘤𝘦𝘴 𝘸𝘩𝘦𝘳𝘦 𝘦𝘷𝘦𝘳𝘺𝘰𝘯𝘦 𝘵𝘩𝘳𝘪𝘷𝘦𝘴. 

⤷ Labels and acronyms may change, specific programs may stop (even better if they evolved!). But fairness, equity, and opportunity must endure. 

And that requires allies among leaders more than ever.

𝐖𝐡𝐚𝐭 𝐜𝐚𝐧 𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐝𝐨?

✔ Recognize our own biases
✔ Challenge biases in decisions and processes
✔ Advocate for fair opportunities
✔ Make inclusion a daily practice, not a buzzword
✔ More important: Make sure all voices are listened to

How are you ensuring your workplace is fair and inclusive? Any other tips I missed?

𝐒𝐨𝐦𝐞 𝐰𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐞𝐫 𝐰𝐨𝐦𝐞𝐧:

I could not end this post without a heartfelt shout-out to inspiring leaders who've shaped my journey:

Caroline Cintra, Leslie Jarrin,  Marcia Alessandra Dos Santos, Marcia Bussolaro, Marcia Tait, Fernanda Dias, Fernanda Pavan Tait, Andrea Aranda, Camila Tartari Kaul, April Johnson, Anita Sands, Roxanne Taylor, Janaina Ruas, Ruth Harrison MBA, Ruth Soliani, Lisa Rathjens, Julie Woods-Moss, Jessie Jie Xia, Rebecca Parsons, Lilian De Munno, Karin Verloop, Ana Rodrigo, Marta Saft Valli, Laura Romero Frías, Mansi Shah, Jackie Kinsey, Joanna Parke, Erin Cummins, Pamela Rampanelli, María Fernanda Escudero Lucero. , PMP, Fu Ying, Ingrid Kelly, Lori Fraleigh, Ramona Mateiu, Jennifer Mounce, Suzi Edwards-Alexander, ...

Thank you for making me a better professional and paving the way for a more inclusive future.

#InternationalWomensDay2025 #InternationalWomensDay #IWD25 #CadenaEJECON y #8marzo2025 

Not to mention all women from EJE&CON Asociación Española de Ejecutiv@s y Consejer@s

PS: The list of some of the leaders I've encountered during my career is just a small fraction of the incredible women who have impacted me. There are many more, but it would not fit a LinkedIn post!
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about allyship in leadership, specifically discussing the importance of dismantling inequality and fostering inclusion, and is likely expressing their thoughts on how leaders can ensure a fair and inclusive workplace. The post also touches on the backlash against DEI initiatives and the need for continued support for diversity, equity, and inclusion.

comments.comment_body: Congratulations, Irina Petrakova-Otto. Success always, no matter where you are. Celebrate the new Journey and new Year.
comments.post_body:
Today marks my last day at Microsoft, and I am filled with a mix of emotions as I share this news. I have decided to embark on a new journey as a founder, and while I am excited about the future, I will always cherish my time here at Microsoft.
For the last 5 years, I have been at the epicenter of the AI revolution, working with amazing people and companies that have created and grown with AI. These experiences have inspired me to spread my wings and work on my own ideas. Microsoft is a remarkable company, and it is the incredible people who make it so. We sometimes forget that a company is only as good as the individuals who work tirelessly to innovate, dream, and build amazing things. My best memories and most rewarding experiences have been meeting and collaborating with such talented and passionate colleagues and partners.
I want to express my deepest gratitude to each of you for being my mentors, coaches, and friends. Your guidance and support have helped me grow and learn in ways I never imagined. As I start this new chapter, I carry with me the lessons and inspiration you have imparted.
Though our paths may diverge for now, I sincerely hope they will intersect again in the future. Thank you for everything, and I look forward to staying connected and seeing the incredible things you will continue to achieve.
Happy Holidays!!!
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a colleague's announcement of their last day at Microsoft, where they shared their decision to leave and start a new journey as a founder, and expressed gratitude to their colleagues for their support and guidance. The commenter is likely offering well-wishes and congratulations on this new chapter.

comments.post_body:
I am beyond thrilled to announce that I have accepted my dream job! I will be joining Xbox Game Studios as a Security Architect!

Gaming has always been a deep passion of mine. My grandma was the first in our family to have a home PC, and we spent countless hours getting lost in classic point-and-click adventure games like Myst and Riven, printing out walkthroughs when we got stuck. Gaming has been a constant in my life, from playing Super Nintendo to building my first gaming PC in high school, to working at GameStop before dedicating myself to college. Now, I get to secure some of the biggest studio names that have always been close to my heart!

Reflecting on my previous 4-year journey with Microsoft, I am filled with gratitude. I’ve grown immensely as an engineer, navigating the complexities of Azure cloud and collaborating with amazing customers and partners. The lessons I've learned and the experiences we've shared have been instrumental in shaping my career.

To my incredible team members, thank you for your unwavering support, wisdom, and camaraderie. You've made this journey both rewarding and unforgettable. I am eternally grateful for the opportunity to learn and grow with you, and I cherish the memories we've created together.

Here’s to new adventures and securing the future of gaming! 🎮🚀

#NewBeginnings #DreamJob #SecurityArchitect #XboxGameStudios #Grateful #Microsoft #Xbox #MicrosoftSecurity
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about someone accepting their dream job as a Security Architect at Xbox Game Studios, sharing their passion for gaming and expressing gratitude for their previous experience at Microsoft. The commenter is likely congratulating the individual on their new role and wishing them success in their future endeavors.

comments.comment_body: Congratulations, Margaryta Ostapchuk!! You have a brilliant path ahead of you.
comments.post_body:
I am delighted to share that I have successfully completed my MBA 🎓 at the University of Toronto - Rotman School of Management. I embarked on my MBA journey during the pandemic, and in 2022, Russia launched a full-scale invasion of Ukraine, reshaping the world as we know it. Throughout these years, one word has stayed with me: perseverance. 
💡  I gained valuable insights from exceptional professors like Dan Richards, Ing-Haw Cheng, Adrian Saville, Katherine DeCelles, Lisa Hehenberger and many more.  
💡  I had the privilege of learning from CEOs and founders of startups such as Daniel Eberhard@KOHO, Jeff Adamson@Neo Financial, and others that are shaping the financial and other industries in Canada and abroad. 
💡 My educational journey took me to Universidad Torcuato Di Tella 
 in Buenos Aires, Argentina, and Esade Business School in Barcelona, Spain, where I focused on emerging markets and social impact. 
💡 Won a case competition focused on organization design led by Eugene Choi.
💡 Along the way, I honed my leadership and negotiation skills, and enhanced my financial acumen while maintaining a strong focus on AI and innovation. Applying design thinking to business problems enriched my experience.

I want to express my immense gratitude to the Microsoft leadership and the entire team - Sara Williams Spalding, Federica Nocera, Juan Carlos Zuluaga, Kavitha Govindarajulu, Pete Roden - for their unwavering support throughout these three years. I am both happy and ready for new opportunities and challenges. The connections and knowledge I’ve gained will undoubtedly shape the next chapter of my journey. Driven by a bigger purpose and hope, I am motivated to support my colleagues and community.
🌟 Congratulations everyone who is just starting educational journey this autumn and best wishes for your future endeavors! 🎓
Today, I donated to the savED Foundation, which helps to rebuild war-damaged schools and supports Ukrainian kids, so they can continue their education during blackouts. Link to the foundation in the comments.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is congratulating the author on completing their MBA at the University of Toronto's Rotman School of Management, and is likely responding to the author's expression of gratitude and enthusiasm for new opportunities. The commenter is acknowledging the author's perseverance and accomplishments throughout their MBA journey.

comments.post_body:
Today is the end of my 12th week journey. All I can feel is joy and satisfaction for taking the risk and trying something different. 

Moving to a new country and exploring a new industry is definitely a life experience.🌎🖥💻

Amazon Web Services (AWS) gave me the chance to confirm that anything is possible in this world if we do have a purpose. Ambiguity is like the chaos we need to go through to get to the order and the rewards. 

Huge thanks to my mentor Lori Petersson for your guidance, to Lindsay Chung for your feedback,  to Nathan Kling for all your efforts and pushing us to do our best, to Jorge Galván. PMP. AWS Cloud Practitioner. for your help on the project and Michael Pabon for your support. 

En buena hora! 😊happy to be an Amazonian

comments.comment_body: Joey Cruz 🤖you have a great team. Congratulations!!!
comments.post_body:
Yesterday I had the honor and pleasure of being a panelist in a University of Washington (UW) event promoted by SOMOS.tech 👩🏽‍💻 (We are tech) and Information School at UW.
We discussed about professional journey, and shared our interview insights and tips with the students who attended the event. 
 
I was very impressed and happy to see the engagement and enthusiasm of the students. They asked thoughtful and relevant questions and showed a genuine interest in learning more about how to pursue a career in this exciting and fast-growing area. 

I was also delighted to connect with my fellow panelists, Audrey Long, specialist in security @ Microsoft, and Victoria Wang, Product Manager @ Microsoft.  who are amazing professionals and role models in their respective areas of expertise.

I enjoyed sharing some interview tips that I learned in my career. These tips can help anyone who is looking for their ideal job. Here are some of them:

- Be yourself and honest during interviews. Focus on your strengths and eagerness to learn and grow.

- Do more than classes and college assignments to stand out. Show your passion and curiosity, collaborate with your colleagues in your own and their projects.
 
- Don't be scared of joining hackathons if you don't know the techs involved. Join them to learn and connect with people. You don't need to be an expert, you just need to be open-minded and willing to learn.

- Be proud and confident of your identity and culture, whether it's LATINX or another. Diversity is a strength, not a weakness. Share your stories and experiences that make you who you are and what you value.

- Experiment with and create your own projects using AI technologies. This will help you learn faster and keep up to date. There are many tools and platforms that you can use to try AI. Your College probably have some Azure credits for students (as Audrey mentioned well). Solve real-world problems that matter to you, or just have fun with AI and see what you can make. 

- Ask ChatGPT or Copilot to review your resume or help you prepare for an interview.

- In an interview, show concrete examples of how you collaborate with your peers at College or at your internship. Talk about one or two examples, pick your best collaboration case. Highlight the outcomes of your collaboration, and how you helped the group learn and succeed.

- To avoid sounding arrogant, when talking about your achievements and what you did, start with what your group did together and then move to the points where you made a difference and helped the group learn and succeed. This will show your humility, initiative and teamwork skills.

Thank you Luz A Mendoza García, Maho Pacheco, Lindsay Sullivan and everyone involved in the organization of the event.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about a University of Washington event where the author shared their professional journey and interview tips with students, and is likely praising the event and the advice given. The post discussed the importance of being oneself, learning, and embracing diversity in pursuing a career in tech.

comments.post_body:
**Shorter story**

I will use part of my sabbatical to mentor and support other professionals on MentorCruise. 

Are you navigating career transitions, looking to enhance your skill set, needing to inspire and create high-performance teams, or simply needing guidance to unlock your full potential? We can work on it together.

Here is the link:
https://lnkd.in/efzYRjbQ 

**Longer story**

Now that the travel part of my sabbatical is over, the daughter is back to school, and I'll spend the remaining sabbatical time at home until the second semester. So, I've decided to dedicate a part of this time to something I'm passionate about—coaching and mentoring professionals and fellow industry enthusiasts. 

This is a great opportunity for me to extend the boundaries of mentorship beyond my organization and connect with individuals from diverse backgrounds and experiences.

Why am I doing this? Throughout my career, mentorship has been crucial to my growth and success. It would be impossible to name them all, but to name a few that helped me in different parts of my career, I will start by recognizing and thanking one important mentor for each different phase of my journey so far: Marcia Bussolaro, Marcia Santos, Jefferson Silva, Vinicius Pagano, Andre Guarido, Leonardo Lamarca, Caroline Cintra, Jackeline Leal, Leslie Jarrin, Karin Verloop, Gabriel Notari, Eduardo Fayh, Mariana Pimenta. You all rock, thank you!

Eventually, I learned how to do it myself. And I have done it often and successfully for years now with my team members and inside internal global leadership programs within my company. Why not also use the time to do it outside of my teams?

So, whether you're navigating career transitions, looking to enhance your skill set, or simply needing guidance to unlock your full potential, I'm here to help.

[EDIT] If you live in a country in which you do not spend on Euros or Dollars, or if for any reason you feel need to talk about costs, DM me. We can arrange something that works for everyone

#Mentorship #CareerGrowth #SabbaticalJourney #MentorCruise #ProfessionalDevelopment

PS.: The image of this post is AI-generated (Dall-E, 2024)
comments.context_summary: The commenter is offering their time to mentor and support other professionals on MentorCruise during their sabbatical, and is inviting those navigating career transitions or seeking guidance to reach out. They are sharing their own experience of being mentored and now wanting to pay it forward and help others unlock their full potential.

comments.comment_body: Congratulations, Omar Abbosh! Thank you for all these years of leaderahip and I wish you all the very best at Pearson.
comments.post_body:
Dear All,  You may have seen some news this morning about the next chapter of my journey. After a few amazing years at Microsoft, I’ve accepted a new role as CEO of Pearson, the world’s leading #learning company. 
 
I believe deeply in the transformative power of learning and education in our ever-changing, #technology driven world. That’s why Pearson’s purpose- to add life to a lifetime of learning- genuinely speaks to my heart. 
 
I’ll be joining Pearson in the new year, which gives me plenty of time to say a proper goodbye to my Microsoft colleagues, partners, and customers. I truly value each of you and am so proud of all we’ve accomplished together. 
 
As I look to the future, I’m excited to help Pearson grow and to play a part in the vital work it does helping people make progress in their lives. I look forward to connecting with many of you before I leave Microsoft and meeting my new Pearson colleagues very soon!
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to the post author's announcement of their new role as CEO of Pearson, a leading learning company, after leaving their position at Microsoft. The author is expressing their excitement for this new chapter and gratitude for their time at Microsoft, and the commenter is likely offering congratulations or well wishes.

comments.comment_body: Parabéns Fabio Scopeta! Fantástica experiência e demonstração de determinação.
comments.post_body:
Three years ago, I found myself asking, "Can I pursue a Master's Degree at one of the world's top universities at this point in my life and career?"

I decided to take on the challenge and today I celebrated my graduation from UC Berkeley, with Steve Wozniak speaking at our commencement! This journey has been nothing short of incredible, and I'm grateful for the growth and opportunities it has brought me.

I'd like to express my deepest gratitude to everyone who supported me throughout this experience—my family, friends, and the inspiring professors. Your encouragement and guidance have truly made all the difference.

Here's to embracing challenges, chasing dreams, and continually striving for personal and professional growth. I'm excited to see where this next chapter leads. Cheers to new beginnings!



#ucberkeley  #Graduation #MastersDegree #Growth #Gratitude

University of California, Berkeley
